title: Author Portfolio
text: This section highlights creative and narrative-centered projects, including interactive experiences published as static sites.

title: Author Portfolio
text:
Featured Projects

Featured Project: Manna, Sarcasm, & Burritos
A broken man, a suspiciously important cat, and a portal into a world where magic, meaning, and survival are far more entangled than they first appear.
Manna, Sarcasm, & Burritos is my Royal Road science-fantasy serial: a portal-fantasy, LitRPG-adjacent story about grief, dislocation, strange worlds, magical systems, and the occasional burrito-shaped survival goal. The story follows Markus, a man at the edge of collapse, whose choice to become a paid test subject leaves him face-to-face with another reality, and a cat who may have been orchestrating far more than he realized.
The project blends humor, wonder, danger, and layered worldbuilding with a deeper interest in meaning, transformation, and the blurry borders between science, magic, and theology.
As a featured project, it represents an important part of my development as a speculative writer: the place where voice, worldbuilding, metaphysical curiosity, and genre play all start colliding in public. It is one of the clearest expressions of the kind of fiction I want to write: weird, thoughtful, emotionally charged, and deeply interested in what happens when ordinary people collide with realities larger than themselves.
Read on Royal Road

title: Darrell Wolfe
text:
Stories, systems, scripture, spreadsheets, and the strange overlap between them.
I’m a storyteller who works across creative writing, biblical studies, data storytelling, data analysis, and personal essays. If that sounds like an odd mix, that is exactly the point: I care about meaning, pattern, structure, and story wherever they show up.

Creative Writing Biblical Studies Data Nerd Personal

title: Darrell Wolfe
text:
Start Here
Welcome. This site is my central online home, not a side project for one topic.
For a long time, different parts of my work lived in different places: one place for writing, one place for biblical studies, one place for data work, one place for personal reflection. It looked organized, but it felt disconnected. The truth is these parts were never separate for me, and I no longer wanted separate homes pretending they were.

title: Darrell Wolfe
text:
Browse by Section


Creative Writing
Short fiction, worldbuilding, strange ideas that refused to stay quiet, and experiments where voice and structure matter as much as plot.


Biblical Studies
Reading scripture with context, language, and careful attention, not hot takes. Notes, reflections, and long-form biblical studies that take the text seriously.


Data Nerd
Data storytelling and data analysis through SQL, dashboards, visual patterns, and practical write-ups that turn messy numbers into useful decisions.


Personal
Personal essays on growth, grief, learning, faith, and everyday life; less polished brand voice, more honest field notes from a real human.

title: Darrell Wolfe
text:
Why This Site Exists
This is one home with different rooms.
I’m not trying to maintain separate identities. I’m trying to do integrated work and live an integrated life. Creative writing sharpens how I tell a story. Biblical studies sharpens how I read meaning. Data work sharpens how I see pattern and structure. Personal writing keeps all of it honest.
If any of that resonates, explore the room that fits you best and wander from there.

title: A Modern Beit Midrash: Engaging Scripture in Community
text:
I’ve had a dream of starting something akin to Beit Midrash (בית מדרש), House of Study.
I want something of a smash between an A.A. meeting and a Book Study, where we come to hear the biblical texts read and interpreted in their own ancient contexts, focus on what those contexts were, and then ask as a group what wisdom was operating for them back then, and as a group (in group discussion, not sermon) come to some conclusion about what wisdom it might have for us today.
All the while, there is food for everyone (actual meals, not a thimble of grape juice) and places for kids to play, and song times campfire style. And then throughout the week, the facility (or homes or coffee shops or whatever) would be places where people can gather in smaller groups or 1:1 to do life together, share struggles, help each other accomplish things.
If someone needs a new roof, we all go make it happen as a group. If we hear of someone in the larger community needing help, we go help, regardless of whether they’re “one of us” or not.
We regularly find the most needy in the region and meet their real needs in meaningful ways, not in token ways. Etc.
It’s ironic, because I’m the least likely person to (1) start anything like this and (2) go find other humans to be human with. But the dream won’t die, it only grows every year.
If anyone wants to start this, I’ll help. 🙂
Just thinking out loud.
Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller.
